title: Scaffold project and implement domain entities + SOLID source contracts
description:
## Goal
Set up the Flutter project structure and implement the foundational domain layer (entities, value objects, source contracts, repository interface, exchange capability registry) following SOLID principles and TDD.

## Effort Estimate
8 hours (one focused implementation slice).

## Implementation
1. Scaffold Flutter project
   - Create lib/ folder structure: core/, domain/, data/, presentation/, ui/.
   - Add pubspec.yaml dependencies: flutter_riverpod, riverpod, http, web_socket_channel, candlesticks, fl_chart, drift, sqlite3_flutter_libs, local_auth, flutter_secure_storage, go_router, freezed_annotation, json_annotation.
   - Add dev dependencies: build_runner, freezed, json_serializable, drift_dev, flutter_lints, mocktail.

2. Define domain value objects and entities (TDD)
   - lib/domain/entities/symbol.dart: base/quote assets, normalised id, exchange-specific rawSymbol.
   - lib/domain/entities/venue.dart: enum or sealed class for binance, bybit, okx, coinbase.
   - lib/domain/entities/ticker.dart: lastPrice, bid, ask, change24h, change24hPercent, volume, timestamp.
   - lib/domain/entities/candle.dart: OHLCV + timestamp.
   - lib/domain/entities/order_book.dart: bids/asks lists + spread.
   - lib/domain/entities/trade.dart: price, amount, side, time.
   - Use freezed/immutable value objects with equality and toString.

3. Define SOLID source contracts
   - lib/domain/sources/ticker_source.dart: Future<Result<Ticker>> fetchTicker(Symbol s).
   - lib/domain/sources/candle_source.dart: Future<Result<List<Candle>>> fetchCandles(Symbol s, Timeframe tf, {int? limit}).
   - lib/domain/sources/order_book_source.dart: Future<Result<OrderBook>> fetchOrderBook(Symbol s, {int? depth}).
   - lib/domain/sources/trade_source.dart: Future<Result<List<Trade>>> fetchTrades(Symbol s, {int? limit}).
   - lib/domain/sources/market_stream_source.dart: Stream<Result<TickerUpdate>>, Stream<Result<OrderBookUpdate>>, Stream<Result<Trade>> watch... methods.

4. Define repository interface and registry
   - lib/domain/repositories/market_data_repository.dart: abstract methods returning Result for sync operations and Result streams for live data.
   - lib/domain/registry/exchange_capability.dart: which features each venue supports (REST ticker, WS order book, etc.).

5. Implement core result type and failures
   - lib/core/result.dart: sealed Result<T> with Success and Failure; map, flatMap, fold helpers.
   - lib/core/failures.dart: NetworkFailure, ParseFailure, UnsupportedFeatureFailure, UnknownFailure.

6. Unit tests
   - test/domain/entities/symbol_test.dart
     - test_symbol_normalization_lowercase_input_converted_to_uppercase
     - test_symbol_invalid_characters_rejected
   - test/domain/entities/ticker_test.dart
     - test_ticker_spread_computed_as_ask_minus_bid
     - test_ticker_negative_price_rejected_or_documented
   - test/core/result_test.dart
     - test_result_map_transforms_success_value
     - test_result_map_preserves_failure
     - test_result_flatMap_short_circuits_on_failure
     - test_result_fold_returns_left_on_failure

## Success Criteria
- [ ] flutter pub get succeeds and all dependencies resolve.
- [ ] lib/ folder structure follows SOLID layers with no files in wrong layer.
- [ ] All domain entities are immutable freezed classes with equality.
- [ ] All source contracts return Result<T> and streams return Result<T> so failures are explicit.
- [ ] Result and Failure types compile and all 6+ unit tests pass.
- [ ] flutter analyze returns zero issues.
- [ ] flutter format --set-exit-if-changed lib test exits 0.
- [ ] No UI, REST, WebSocket, or exchange-specific code exists yet.

## Key Considerations
**Symbol normalization edge cases:**
- Inputs may be mixed case (btc/usdt) or already upper case (BTC/USDT); normalize to uppercase internally.
- Reject symbols containing whitespace or empty strings; use const assertion in tests.
- Preserve exchange-specific raw symbol (e.g. BTC-USDT for Coinbase, BTCUSDT for Binance) without leaking it into generic logic.

**Value-object equality:**
- Two Tickers with identical field values must be equal; two with different timestamps must not be equal.
- Equality on doubles must handle small precision differences via fixed-decimal types if available, otherwise document that exact-bit equality is required.

**Result type design:**
- Prefer explicit Result over exceptions for expected failures (network, parse, unsupported feature).
- UnknownFailure is for truly unexpected errors and must carry stack trace or original error.

**Freezed + code generation:**
- Run build_runner once to verify code generation works; commit generated files if team convention requires.
- If freezed causes analyzer issues, ensure build_runner output is included in analysis.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ No mutable entity classes (domain objects must be immutable).
- ❌ No throwing exceptions from source contracts (use Result).
- ❌ No UI or exchange-specific code in domain layer.
- ❌ No TODO stubs without a linked br issue number.
- ❌ No tautological tests that only verify constructors or field existence.
close_reason: Completed: scaffolded project, implemented domain entities, Result type, failures, source contracts, repository interface, and exchange capability registry. All tests, analyze, and format pass.

title: Implement local biometric/PIN auth guard
design:
## Goal
Implement a local biometric/PIN authentication guard that blocks access to the app's main content until the user authenticates. The guard must work offline, use the platform's biometric APIs through the local_auth plugin, and fall back to a PIN when biometrics are unavailable or fail.

## Effort Estimate
6–8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `flutter analyze` returns no errors or warnings.
- [ ] `flutter test` passes all new and existing tests.
- [ ] `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` exits cleanly.
- [ ] `LocalAuthService` interface is abstract and lives in `lib/domain/auth/`; implementation lives in `lib/data/auth/` and depends only on `local_auth`.
- [ ] `AuthNotifier` exposes a sealed/enum `AuthState` and transitions through `unknown`, `authenticated`, `unauthenticated`, and `error` states.
- [ ] `AuthGateScreen` blocks the main app content until `AuthState.authenticated` is emitted; shows a biometric button, PIN fallback input, and human-readable error messages.
- [ ] `lib/main.dart` wraps the app so the gate is the first screen shown.
- [ ] Tests verify notifier state transitions for: biometric available/success, biometric failure, biometric cancellation, PIN success, PIN failure, and sign-out.
- [ ] Widget test verifies gate shows blocked content before auth and replaces with app content after successful auth.
- [ ] No API secrets, PINs, or biometric data are persisted; only a runtime auth flag is held in notifier state.

## Implementation Checklist

### Domain layer (`lib/domain/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/domain/auth/auth_failure.dart` with failure subtypes:
  - `BiometricNotAvailableFailure`
  - `AuthCancelledFailure`
  - `AuthFailedFailure`
  - `PinNotSetFailure`
  - `PinMismatchFailure`
- [ ] Create `lib/domain/auth/local_auth_service.dart` abstract interface:
  - `Future<Result<bool>> authenticate()`
  - `Future<bool> canCheckBiometrics()`

### Data layer (`lib/data/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/data/auth/local_auth_service_impl.dart` implementing `LocalAuthService`.
- [ ] Use `local_auth` `LocalAuthentication`.
- [ ] On success return `Success(true)`.
- [ ] Map plugin exceptions/cancellations to appropriate `AuthFailure`.

### Presentation layer (`lib/presentation/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_state.dart` with `AuthState` enum/sealed class: `unknown`, `unauthenticated`, `authenticated`, `error(Failure)`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_notifier.dart` extending `StateNotifier<AuthState>`.
- [ ] Methods: `checkBiometricAvailability()`, `authenticateWithBiometrics()`, `authenticateWithPin(String pin)`, `signOut()`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_guard_provider.dart` exposing the notifier provider.

### UI layer (`lib/ui/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/auth/auth_gate_screen.dart` as full-screen gate.
- [ ] Watch auth state; show loading for `unknown`, auth controls for `unauthenticated`, app content for `authenticated`, and error message for `error`.
- [ ] Provide biometric button, PIN text field, submit button, and sign-out button when authenticated.

### Integration (`lib/main.dart`)
- [ ] Update `main.dart` to show `AuthGateScreen` as `home` or initial route, with the real app content as its child.

### Tests
- [ ] Create `test/presentation/auth/auth_notifier_test.dart` using mocktail for `LocalAuthService`.
- [ ] Create `test/ui/auth/auth_gate_screen_test.dart` using `ProviderScope` overrides.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Biometric unavailable**
- `canCheckBiometrics()` returns false on simulators or unsupported devices.
- UI must still show PIN fallback; notifier does not emit an error just because biometrics are unavailable.

**Edge Case: Biometric cancelled by user**
- Plugin may return false or throw `PlatformException` for cancellation.
- Map to `AuthCancelledFailure` and emit `AuthState.error` without locking the user out permanently.

**Edge Case: PIN input**
- Empty PIN should be treated as mismatch, not crash.
- Hard-code a demo PIN constant (e.g., `"1234"`) for this iteration; do not store it in secure storage or persist it.
- Future iteration can replace with secure storage-backed PIN.

**Edge Case: No initial state**
- `AuthState.unknown` should show a loading indicator; do not auto-trigger biometric on first build to avoid permission dialogs during widget tests.
- Notifier constructor starts at `unknown`; caller explicitly invokes `checkBiometricAvailability()` or authentication.

**Edge Case: Sign out**
- `signOut()` returns state to `unauthenticated`; gate reappears.

**Concurrency**
- All async methods should be awaited; no parallel auth requests should corrupt state. StateNotifier's synchronous state setter naturally serializes updates.

**Security**
- Do not log the PIN.
- Do not persist auth state; it is runtime-only.
- Do not store actual PINs or secrets.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases.
- ❌ No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- ❌ No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- ❌ No `unwrap`/`expect`-style assumptions on plugin results.
- ❌ No hardcoded secrets beyond a single demo PIN constant.
- ❌ No testing of real biometric APIs; mock `LocalAuthService` in unit/widget tests.

## Verification Commands
```bash
export PATH=\"\$PATH:/Users/deepol/development/flutter/bin\"
flutter analyze
flutter test
dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test
```

title: Build Exchange Management screen for read-only API key input
description:
## Goal
Implement the Exchange Management screen where users can add, edit, and remove read-only exchange API keys for Binance, Bybit, OKX, and Coinbase. Keys are stored securely; only read-only public endpoints are exercised for validation.

## Implementation
1. Domain additions
   - Add  value object: venue, apiKey, secret, password, isEnabled, createdAt.
   - Add  interface: save, load, delete, validate, list enabled venues.
   - Add failure types: InvalidCredentialsFailure, SecureStorageFailure.

2. Secure storage data source
   - lib/data/datasources/local/secure_credentials_store.dart
   - Use  with key namespaced by venue id.
   - Never log values; write tests with a mock store.

3. Validation use case
   - lib/domain/usecases/validate_exchange_credentials.dart
   - Call private read-only balance endpoint for the venue using the provided credentials.
   - Return Result<bool>; do not persist balance data.

4. Presentation
   - Add  and .
   - Add  for test-connection state.

5. UI
   - lib/ui/screens/exchange_management_screen.dart: list of venues with status.
   - lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_edit_screen.dart or modal: API key/secret inputs, enabled toggle, test connection, delete.
   - Use obscured text fields with reveal toggle.
   - Show permission hint: read-only keys recommended.

6. Tests
   - test/domain/usecases/validate_exchange_credentials_test.dart
   - test/data/datasources/local/secure_credentials_store_test.dart (mocked)
   - test/presentation/exchange_credentials_notifier_test.dart
   - widget test for ExchangeManagementScreen navigation and form.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] User can enable/disable each venue and save preference.
- [ ] API key and secret are stored in secure storage and never logged.
- [ ] Test connection calls a read-only endpoint and reports success/failure.
- [ ] Invalid/empty credentials produce a clear error without crashing.
- [ ] Delete removes keys and disables venue.
- [ ] All new code follows SOLID layers and passes flutter analyze/format/test.

## Key Considerations
**Security:**
- Keys must be trimmed but never exposed in logs or UI beyond reveal toggle.
- Warn if user enters keys with trading/withdrawal permissions (detect via exchange permissions endpoint where available).
- Secure storage migration handled by  v10 cipher defaults.

**Edge cases:**
- No secure storage available (emulator/simulator) → graceful error message.
- User cancels biometric during reveal → keep field obscured.
- Test connection fails due to network → distinguish from invalid key.
- Multiple venues saved → list loads asynchronously per venue.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ No storing keys in SharedPreferences or plain text.
- ❌ No sending keys to analytics or crash reporting.
- ❌ No writing real credentials in tests.
- ❌ No full trading permissions required or assumed.
- ❌ No TODO stubs without linked br issue.

See also:
- [docs/screens.md](../docs/screens.md) for screen flows and states.
- [docs/architecture.md](../docs/architecture.md) for overall architecture.
- [AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md) for project conventions and XP/SOLID rules.

title: Implement Binance REST and WebSocket market data client
close_reason: Completed: BinanceRestClient and BinanceWebSocketClient with adapters for ticker, candles, order book, trades; all endpoints tested with mocked HTTP/WebSocket.

title: Implement Riverpod providers for market data streams
design:
## Goal
Refactor MarketDataRepositoryImpl so it can route market-data requests to real Binance/Bybit REST/WebSocket clients when configured, while preserving the mock store as an offline/demo fallback. Then add Riverpod providers that expose ticker, candle, order-book, and trade streams as AsyncValue states, plus a repository provider that wires real sources and a selected-symbol provider defaulting to BTC/USDT @ Binance.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `MarketDataRepositoryImpl` accepts `Map<Venue, VenueSources>` without breaking existing constructor callers.
- [ ] When a venue has `VenueSources`, REST and WebSocket methods use the real source; otherwise they fall back to the mock store.
- [ ] Unsupported-feature checks still return `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` before any source/mock is invoked.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart` constructs the repository with real Binance/Bybit REST + WebSocket clients and a static capability registry.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart` defaults to BTC/USDT on Binance.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/market_data_providers.dart` exposes `tickerStreamProvider`, `candlesProvider`, `orderBookStreamProvider`, and `tradesStreamProvider` using `family` with `TradingSymbol`.
- [ ] Providers convert `Result` from the repository into `AsyncData`/`AsyncError` (throw failures so Riverpod wraps them).
- [ ] `test/presentation/providers/market_data_providers_test.dart` verifies AsyncData after `Success` and AsyncError after `Failure` for each provider.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Study `MarketDataRepository`, `MarketDataRepositoryImpl`, exchange source contracts, Binance/Bybit clients, and `ExchangeCapabilityRegistry`.
- [ ] Define `VenueSources` in `lib/data/repositories/market_data_repository_impl.dart` bundling `TickerSource`, `CandleSource`, `OrderBookSource`, `TradeSource`, and `MarketStreamSource`.
- [ ] Refactor `MarketDataRepositoryImpl` constructor to accept optional `Map<Venue, VenueSources> venueSources`.
- [ ] Update each repository method to prefer real source when present and capable, else mock store, else `UnsupportedFeatureFailure`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart` with:
  - A private static registry implementation of `ExchangeCapabilityRegistry` covering Binance and Bybit with all `MarketDataFeature` values.
  - A `marketDataRepositoryProvider` `Provider<MarketDataRepository>` that instantiates `BinanceRestClient`, `BinanceWebSocketClient`, `BybitRestClient`, `BybitWebSocketClient`, and `MockMarketDataStore`, wiring them into `VenueSources` for Binance and Bybit.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart` with a `StateProvider<TradingSymbol>` defaulting to `TradingSymbol(base: 'BTC', quote: 'USDT', venue: Venue.binance, rawSymbol: 'BTCUSDT')`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/market_data_providers.dart` with:
  - `tickerStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<Ticker, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchTicker(...)`.
  - `candlesProvider` (`FutureProvider.family<List<Candle>, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.getCandles(..., Timeframe.h1)`.
  - `orderBookStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<OrderBook, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchOrderBook(...)`.
  - `tradesStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<List<Trade>, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchTrades(...)`.
  - Map each `Result` with `fold(...)` to throw `Failure` on error so Riverpod emits `AsyncError`.
- [ ] Add `test/presentation/providers/market_data_providers_test.dart`:
  - Fake `MarketDataRepository` returning `Success` for all methods.
  - Fake `MarketDataRepository` returning `Failure` for all methods.
  - One test per provider asserting `AsyncData`/`AsyncError`.
- [ ] Run `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test`; fix issues.
- [ ] Commit all changes with the specified message.
- [ ] Close `youtrade-8s9` with the specified reason.

## Key Considerations

### Edge Case: Unsupported Venue
If the registry returns no capability for a venue, or the capability does not support the requested feature, the repository must immediately return `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` without touching real or mock sources.

### Edge Case: Source Present but Capability Offline
If `venueSources` contains a venue but the registry says the feature is unsupported, the repository returns `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` rather than using the source. Capability is the gate.

### Edge Case: Real Source Returns Failure
When a real source returns `Err(...)`, propagate that failure directly (do not fall back to mock). The caller/provider decides how to surface it.

### Edge Case: Empty `venueSources`
With `venueSources` null or empty, every supported-capability request falls back to `MockMarketDataStore`. Existing repository tests must continue to pass unchanged.

### Edge Case: Provider Disposal
`StreamProvider.family` will cancel the underlying stream when the provider is no longer listened to. No manual subscription management is required because Riverpod owns the provider lifecycle.

### Concurrency
The repository is stateless; multiple providers can call it concurrently. Real REST clients each own their own `http.Client`. WebSocket streams are created per `watch*` call.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No exchange-specific parsing or URL building in providers or UI.
- ❌ No giant exchange interface; keep source contracts small and segregated.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for repository or provider implementations.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use existing WebSocket streams.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers/providers.
- ❌ No `AsyncValue` exposed as `Result`; providers unwrap `Result`.
- ❌ No direct `http` or `WebSocketChannel` usage outside the data layer.
close_reason: Completed Riverpod providers for market data with real Binance/Bybit wiring and fallback to mock store

title: Phase 4: Build UI screens from mockups
YouTrade Flutter app: multi-venue trading terminal
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Build a Flutter mobile trading terminal called YouTrade with the screens and visual design pixel-for-pixel faithful to /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html.
- Reproduce exactly these screens: Home / Aggregated Portfolio, Trading Terminal, Markets / Screener, Exchange Detail, Options Chain, Compare, Orders & History, Account / Settings.
- Preserve the visual identity: iOS 26 Liquid Glass-style device frame, dark-first palette, Flux (dir:b) and Carbon (dir:a) visual directions, Geist/Space Grotesk/JetBrains Mono typography.
- Reproduce every interactive component from the mockups: iOS status bar, app header with theme/direction toggles, bottom tab navigation, exchange allocation bar, exchange cards, position list, equity curve canvas, symbol chips, candlestick chart with MA7/MA25 and volume, time-frame selector, compare chart, order book with depth bars, trade ticket with buy/sell toggle / order-type chips / leverage slider / size-percent buttons, lower tabs (Trade/Book/Info/Signals), technical-signals gauge, oscillator/MA/pivot tables, fundamentals card, options chain grid, market screener with filters and sparklines, orders tabs (Open/History/Positions), settings list with connected exchanges and appearance toggles.
- Follow SOLID architecture: domain abstractions, per-exchange implementations, Riverpod state layer, dumb UI.
- Support mock-first offline data and real public market data online; no exchange API secrets in the mobile app for this epic.
- Integrate public REST + WebSocket market data from Binance, Bybit, OKX, and Coinbase via direct Flutter calls.
- Add local biometric/PIN gate before portfolio/trading screens; no cloud account required.
- Keep build under ten minutes: flutter test, flutter analyze, flutter format must pass.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All mockup screens exist and are navigable with the same layout, spacing, typography, and colors as /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html.
- [ ] Domain entities, source contracts, repository, and use cases are implemented and unit-tested.
- [ ] At least two exchanges stream real tickers/candles/order books/trades through a unified abstraction.
- [ ] Offline mode displays mock data with a visible Demo/Offline indicator.
- [ ] Theme matches mockups: dark first, Flux/Carbon toggle, iOS-first styling.
- [ ] Local auth gate blocks access until biometric/PIN succeeds.
- [ ] flutter test, flutter analyze, flutter format --set-exit-if-changed lib test pass.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app (security: first build is public data only).
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases (architecture: must go through repository/interfaces).
- No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods (SOLID: interface segregation).
- No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract (completeness: core abstraction must be real).
- No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols (performance: respect rate limits).
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers (state management: emit new state).
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval (design fidelity).

## Approach
Adopt layered/clean architecture driven by SOLID. Domain defines small contracts (TickerSource, CandleSource, OrderBookSource, TradeSource, MarketStreamSource) and a MarketDataRepository. Each exchange provides a REST client, WebSocket client, and adapter that transform exchange DTOs into domain entities. A registry maps venues to clients so new exchanges can be added without editing core logic. Riverpod providers expose AsyncValue/Stream states to dumb UI widgets. Drift caches market data; mock data store provides offline/demo fallback. UI is implemented screen-by-screen to match the mockups, with a shared theme extension and reusable widget kit.

## Child Epics
- youtrade-0z9: Phase 1 - Scaffold + domain entities + SOLID contracts
- youtrade-fl6: Phase 2 - Data layer (mock store, cache, exchange clients)
- youtrade-qqg: Phase 3 - Presentation, theme, and auth guard
- youtrade-mv8: Phase 4 - UI screens from mockups
- youtrade-4hy: Exchange Management screen
- youtrade-75g: Phase 5 - Integration, navigation, and verification

## Architecture
- Domain: entities (Ticker, Candle, OrderBook, Trade, Symbol, Venue), source contracts, MarketDataRepository interface, use cases, ExchangeCapability registry.
- Data: per-exchange REST clients, WebSocket clients, DTOs, adapters, MarketDataRepositoryImpl, Drift cache, mock data store, secure settings.
- Presentation: Riverpod providers and notifiers for tickers, candles, order books, portfolio, theme, auth guard.
- UI: screens matching mockups exactly, reusable widgets, candlestick charts (candlesticks), sparklines (fl_chart).

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The mockups describe a sophisticated multi-venue terminal but contain only synthetic client-side data. We need a real architecture that can graduate from prototype to production without rewriting everything.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html:1-1113 contains the full interactive prototype with screens, theme tokens, component specs, and synthetic data model.
- /mockups/colors_and_type.css:1-301 defines OpenTreasury/YouTrade design tokens (fonts, brand colors, light/dark variables).
- /mockups/ios-frame.jsx:1-349 provides iOS 26 Liquid Glass frame primitives to mirror in Flutter.

**External:**
- CCXT has no Dart/Flutter SDK (docs.ccxt.com, github.com/ccxt/ccxt/issues/4861). Direct public REST/WebSocket calls from Flutter are the chosen path for this epic.
- Binance, Bybit, OKX, Coinbase all expose public REST + WebSocket market data without API keys.
- Recommended packages: riverpod, web_socket_channel, http, candlesticks, fl_chart, drift, local_auth, flutter_secure_storage, go_router.

### Approaches Considered
1. **Direct public REST/WebSocket from Flutter** (chosen)
   - Pros: No backend to run, faster prototype, matches user choice.
   - Cons: Each exchange has different formats; requires adapters.
   - Chosen because: aligns with hybrid prototype + real data scope.

2. **CCXT backend proxy**
   - Pros: Unified, secrets stay server-side.
   - Cons: Requires backend deployment; overkill for public-data-only first build.
   - Rejected because: out of scope for this epic.

3. **Use exchange-specific Dart packages (e.g. babel_binance)**
   - Pros: Less parsing code.
   - Cons: Limited exchange coverage, inconsistent APIs, dependency risk.
   - Rejected because: does not satisfy multi-venue requirement cleanly.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All mockup screens and navigation with the same layout, components, and visual design.
- Domain architecture, per-exchange REST/WebSocket clients, adapters.
- Mock-first offline mode + real online public market data.
- Theme system (Flux/Carbon, dark/light), local auth gate.

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Real trading (create/cancel orders, balances from private APIs).
- User accounts, cloud sync, push notifications.
- Real portfolio P&L from exchange positions.
- Options chain real data (will be mock/synthetic).

### Open Questions
- Which exact timeframes to support for candles? (decide during implementation)
- Should the app support horizontal orientation or stay portrait-only? (decide during implementation)
- How many candles to cache locally? (decide during implementation)
